title: lithocardium macleodii
intro: Lithocardium macleodii, also known as Digitalis purpurea, is a flowering plant that belongs to the foxglove family and is native to Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to increase the force and rate of the heartbeat. Lithocardium macleodii contains cardiac glycosides, which have been used to treat various heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, but it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
